clusters.title: US indicts Mexican state governor over drug trafficking
clusters.neutral_headline: US charges Mexican governor and other leaders with aiding drug cartel
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, BBC World, Al Jazeera and others

items.title: US indicts Mexican state governor over drug trafficking
items.standfirst: The US Department of Justice has charged Rubén Rocha Moya, governor of the Mexican state of Sinaloa, with drug trafficking in a rare move against a sitting foreign official. If convicted, he could face life in prison, as Washington steps up its crackdown on narcotics networks.
items.content: The US Department of Justice has charged Rubén Rocha Moya, governor of the Mexican state of Sinaloa, with drug trafficking in a rare move against a sitting foreign official. If convicted, he could face life in prison, as Washington steps up its crackdown on narcotics networks.

items.title: US charges Mexican governor and other leaders with aiding drug cartel
items.standfirst: The governor of Sinaloa, Rubén Rocha Moya, hails from the same party as the president of Mexico.
items.content: The governor of Sinaloa, Rubén Rocha Moya, hails from the same party as the president of Mexico.

items.title: US indicts Sinaloa governor, 9 others over Mexican drug cartel links
items.standfirst: Indictment alleges that Governor Ruben Rocha Moya and other current and former officials worked with cartel leaders.
items.content: Indictment alleges that Governor Ruben Rocha Moya and other current and former officials worked with cartel leaders.

items.title: US charges Sinaloa governor and other Mexican officials with drug trafficking offences
items.standfirst: Indictment accuses high-level officials in Sinaloa of offences such as drug trafficking, weapons offences and kidnapping The US justice department has charged the governor of Sinaloa and nine other current and former Mexican officials for alleged ties to the Sinaloa cartel, accusing them of aiding in the massive importation of illicit narcotics into the United States .Some officials were members of Mexico’s progressive ruling party, Morena, posing a political conundrum for Mexican president Claudia Sheinbaum as she seeks to offset mounting pressures from the Trump administration. Continue reading...
items.content: Indictment accuses high-level officials in Sinaloa of offences such as drug trafficking, weapons offences and kidnapping The US justice department has charged the governor of Sinaloa and nine other current and former Mexican officials for alleged ties to the Sinaloa cartel, accusing them of aiding in the massive importation of illicit narcotics into the United States .Some officials were members of Mexico’s progressive ruling party, Morena, posing a political conundrum for Mexican president Claudia Sheinbaum as she seeks to offset mounting pressures from the Trump administration. Continue reading...

items.standfirst: The U.S. government has charged the governor of Mexico's Sinaloa state and nine other current and former Mexican officials with drug trafficking and weapons offenses in a federal indictment.
items.content: The U.S. government has charged the governor of Mexico's Sinaloa state and nine other current and former Mexican officials with drug trafficking and weapons offenses in a federal indictment.

items.title: Who are the victims of the stabbings?
items.standfirst: Two Jewish men have been stabbed in Golders Green, north London, an attack police have declared a terrorist incident.
items.content: Two Jewish men have been stabbed in Golders Green, north London, an attack police have declared a terrorist incident.

items.title: Bodycam footage shows police detaining London stabbing suspect
items.standfirst: Body-worn camera video footage shows Metropolitan Police officers detaining a knife-attack suspect at Golders Green in London, home to a large Jewish community. Two men were stabbed outside a synagogue in north London in the wake of a series of recent arson attacks targeting Jewish sites in the area.
items.content: Body-worn camera video footage shows Metropolitan Police officers detaining a knife-attack suspect at Golders Green in London, home to a large Jewish community. Two men were stabbed outside a synagogue in north London in the wake of a series of recent arson attacks targeting Jewish sites in the area.

items.title: London police release bodycam of arrest after two Jewish men stabbed
items.standfirst: Police in the UK have released bodycam footage showing the arrest of a suspect after two Jewish men were stabbed.
items.content: Police in the UK have released bodycam footage showing the arrest of a suspect after two Jewish men were stabbed.

items.title: Two Jewish men stabbed in London
items.standfirst: A 45-year-old suspect has been arrested after stabbing two Jewish men in North London. New video shows the dramatic moment officers took the suspect down. NBC News’ Daniele Hamamdjian reports from the scene.
items.content: A 45-year-old suspect has been arrested after stabbing two Jewish men in North London. New video shows the dramatic moment officers took the suspect down. NBC News’ Daniele Hamamdjian reports from the scene.

items.title: Watch: Met Police body-worn footage of Golders Green arrest
items.standfirst: Footage shows the moment a man was arrested after two Jewish men were stabbed in north London.
items.content: Footage shows the moment a man was arrested after two Jewish men were stabbed in north London.

items.title: Two wounded as knife attacker targets London’s Jewish community
items.standfirst: U.K. police declared a stabbing attack that injured two Jewish people in London was a "terrorist incident." A 45-year-old man with a history of mental health issues stabbed and seriously wounded two Jewish men in Golders Green, North London.
items.content: U.K. police declared a stabbing attack that injured two Jewish people in London was a "terrorist incident." A 45-year-old man with a history of mental health issues stabbed and seriously wounded two Jewish men in Golders Green, North London.

items.title: Suspect arrested in London terrorist incident in which two Jewish people were stabbed

items.content: Metropolitan Police say they have arrested a 45-year-old man in connection with a stabbing attack that injured two Jewish people in London, which they called a “terrorist incident.”

items.title: Iran war pushes oil prices to 4-year high as Hegseth to face lawmakers

items.content: Oil prices hit a 4-year high as Axios reports Trump will hear new options to try to break the Strait of Hormuz standoff with Iran with a new wave of attacks.

items.title: Iran warns Trump's blockade is 'doomed to fail' as energy prices soar to 4-year high
items.standfirst: Iran warned President Trump's naval blockade was “doomed to fail” as oil prices soared to their highest point in years with the standoff over the Strait of Hormuz wreaking havoc on the global economy.
items.content: Iran warned President Trump's naval blockade was “doomed to fail” as oil prices soared to their highest point in years with the standoff over the Strait of Hormuz wreaking havoc on the global economy.

items.title: Strait of Hormuz: Iran-US standoff drags on
items.standfirst: Donald Trump says a US naval blockade on Iran could last for months, as the standoff between Washington and Tehran continues. The crisis has disrupted oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz, sending prices to multi-year highs. FRANCE 24's correspondent in Tehran, Reza Sayah, has more.
items.content: Donald Trump says a US naval blockade on Iran could last for months, as the standoff between Washington and Tehran continues. items.title: Press freedom worldwide is at lowest level in 25 years, watchdog group warns
items.standfirst: Press freedom across the globe has fallen to its lowest level in a quarter of a century, Reporters Without Borders warned.
items.content: Press freedom across the globe has fallen to its lowest level in a quarter of a century, Reporters Without Borders warned.

items.title: Press freedom worldwide falls to its lowest level in 25 years
items.standfirst: Reporters Without Borders warns that 'journalism is increasingly criminalised worldwide'.
items.content: Reporters Without Borders warns that 'journalism is increasingly criminalised worldwide'.

items.title: Global press freedom falls to lowest level in 25 years, RSF warns
items.standfirst: Freedom of the press has fallen to its lowest level in a quarter of a century, NGO Reporters without Borders (RSF) warned Thursday as it released its annual global ranking. The group reported a worldwide decline in media freedom, citing factors ranging from US President Donald Trump’s “systematic” attacks on the press to actions in Saudi Arabia, where a journalist was executed in 2025.
items.content: Freedom of the press has fallen to its lowest level in a quarter of a century, NGO Reporters without Borders (RSF) warned Thursday as it released its annual global ranking. The group reported a worldwide decline in media freedom, citing factors ranging from US President Donald Trump’s “systematic” attacks on the press to actions in Saudi Arabia, where a journalist was executed in 2025.

clusters.title: Crude oil prices hit four-year high as Iran war threatens to drag on
clusters.neutral_headline: Oil price tops $126 a barrel after Trump warns Iran blockade could last ‘months’
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, Guardian World, Guardian Business

items.title: Crude oil prices hit four-year high as Iran war threatens to drag on
items.standfirst: Brent crude has jumped to its highest level since the start of the Ukraine war after US President Donald Trump signalled that the American blockade on the Strait of Hormuz could last for months. Also in this edition: the US Federal Reserve prepares for a new chief as its board tries to maintain political independence, and French oil giant TotalEnergies faces calls for a windfall tax amid soaring profits.
items.content: Brent crude has jumped to its highest level since the start of the Ukraine war after US President Donald Trump signalled that the American blockade on the Strait of Hormuz could last for months. Also in this edition: the US Federal Reserve prepares for a new chief as its board tries to maintain political independence, and French oil giant TotalEnergies faces calls for a windfall tax amid soaring profits.

items.title: Oil price tops $126 a barrel after Trump warns Iran blockade could last ‘months’
items.standfirst: Markets spooked as US president appears willing to keep up naval blockade and Iran keeps Hormuz strait all but shutBusiness live – latest updatesThe global oil price has soared above $126 a barrel, its highest level since 2022, after Donald Trump warned the US blockade of Iranian ports could last for months and peace talks remained stalled.After surging more than 13% in 24 hours, the price of Brent crude futures reached its highest price since the war began on 28 February. Not since Russia’s 2022 invasion of Ukraine has Brent topped $120, with the price then peaking at $139. Continue reading...
items.content: Markets spooked as US president appears willing to keep up naval blockade and Iran keeps Hormuz strait all but shutBusiness live – latest updatesThe global oil price has soared above $126 a barrel, its highest level since 2022, after Donald Trump warned the US blockade of Iranian ports could last for months and peace talks remained stalled.After surging more than 13% in 24 hours, the price of Brent crude futures reached its highest price since the war began on 28 February. Not since Russia’s 2022 invasion of Ukraine has Brent topped $120, with the price then peaking at $139. Continue reading...

items.title: US gas prices surge to highest level in four years, averaging $4.18 a gallon
items.standfirst: Oil prices surged as talks on reopening of strait of Hormuz remain gridlocked, sending prices $1 higher than last yearUS gas prices rose to their highest level in four years on Thursday, reaching an average $4.18 a gallon at the pump as US-Israeli peace talks with Iran remain at a standstill.The last time average US gas prices breached $4.15 a gallon was in April 2022, when oil prices soared shortly after Russia invaded Ukraine. Average gas prices are now $1 higher than just a year ago, when they were closer to $3.15 a gallon. Continue reading...
items.content: Oil prices surged as talks on reopening of strait of Hormuz remain gridlocked, sending prices $1 higher than last yearUS gas prices rose to their highest level in four years on Thursday, reaching an average $4.18 a gallon at the pump as US-Israeli peace talks with Iran remain at a standstill.The last time average US gas prices breached $4.15 a gallon was in April 2022, when oil prices soared shortly after Russia invaded Ukraine. Average gas prices are now $1 higher than just a year ago, when they were closer to $3.15 a gallon. Continue reading...

clusters.title: US: Supreme Court weakens voting rigths act
clusters.neutral_headline: US: Supreme Court weakens voting rigths act
bullet_summary: In a three-to-six decision, the court said the new map constituted racial gerrymandering
Reported by France24, NPR News, CBS News

items.title: US: Supreme Court weakens voting rigths act
items.standfirst: The Supreme Court of the United States struck down a majority-Black congressional district in Louisiana, ruling it relied too heavily on race and weakening protections under Voting Rights Act of 1965. The 6–3 decision, driven by the court’s conservative majority, is expected to make it harder for minority communities to challenge discriminatory maps, with critics warning it could reduce representation and benefit Republicans in future elections. FRANCE 24's international affairs editor Kethevane Gorjestani has more as midterm elections are approaching.
items.content: The Supreme Court of the United States struck down a majority-Black congressional district in Louisiana, ruling it relied too heavily on race and weakening protections under Voting Rights Act of 1965. The 6–3 decision, driven by the court’s conservative majority, is expected to make it harder for minority communities to challenge discriminatory maps, with critics warning it could reduce representation and benefit Republicans in future elections. FRANCE 24's international affairs editor Kethevane Gorjestani has more as midterm elections are approaching.

items.title: Supreme Court paves the way for largest-ever drop in Black representation in Congress
items.standfirst: By weakening Voting Rights Act protections against racial discrimination in redistricting, the Supreme Court has paved the way for the largest-ever drop in representation by Black members of Congress.
items.content: By weakening Voting Rights Act protections against racial discrimination in redistricting, the Supreme Court has paved the way for the largest-ever drop in representation by Black members of Congress.(Image credit: J. Scott Applewhite)

items.title: Supreme Court voting rights case: Breakdown, impact and reaction
items.standfirst: The Supreme Court handed down a ruling on Wednesday, weakening the Voting Rights Act. The case was about a Louisiana congressional map that added a second majority-Black district to the state. In a three-to-six decision, the court said the new map constituted racial gerrymandering. CBS News' Jessica Levinson and David Becker break down the ruling and Rep. Troy Carter of Louisiana shares his reaction.
items.content: The Supreme Court handed down a ruling on Wednesday, weakening the Voting Rights Act. The case was about a Louisiana congressional map that added a second majority-Black district to the state. In a three-to-six decision, the court said the new map constituted racial gerrymandering. CBS News' Jessica Levinson and David Becker break down the ruling and Rep. Troy Carter of Louisiana shares his reaction.

items.title: Christchurch mass killer loses bid to overturn conviction
items.standfirst: White supremacist Brenton Tarrant's appeal is "utterly devoid of merit", a New Zealand court has ruled.
items.content: White supremacist Brenton Tarrant's appeal is "utterly devoid of merit", a New Zealand court has ruled.

items.title: Christchurch gunman fails in bid to appeal against guilty pleas in New Zealand court
items.standfirst: Australian white supremacist who murdered 51 Muslims said poor mental health made him admit to crimesThe Australian white supremacist who murdered 51 Muslim worshippers at two mosques in Christchurch in 2019 has been prevented from appealing against his guilty pleas, after one of New Zealand’s highest courts said his bid was “utterly devoid of merit”.Brenton Tarrant, who is responsible for the worst mass shooting in New Zealand’s history, asked the court of appeal in February to allow him to appeal against his guilty pleas, claiming harsh prison conditions had affected his mental health and compelled him to admit to the crimes. Continue reading...
items.content: Australian white supremacist who murdered 51 Muslims said poor mental health made him admit to crimesThe Australian white supremacist who murdered 51 Muslim worshippers at two mosques in Christchurch in 2019 has been prevented from appealing against his guilty pleas, after one of New Zealand’s highest courts said his bid was “utterly devoid of merit”.Brenton Tarrant, who is responsible for the worst mass shooting in New Zealand’s history, asked the court of appeal in February to allow him to appeal against his guilty pleas, claiming harsh prison conditions had affected his mental health and compelled him to admit to the crimes. items.title: Powell to remain on Fed board after term ends, preventing Trump appointment
items.standfirst: US Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell on Wednesday urged central bank independence as he nears the end of his term on May 15. He said he would remain a Fed governor, drawing criticism from President Donald Trump, who mocked the decision.
items.content: US Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell on Wednesday urged central bank independence as he nears the end of his term on May 15. He said he would remain a Fed governor, drawing criticism from President Donald Trump, who mocked the decision.

items.title: Powell to stay on Federal Reserve board as policy makers hold rates steady
items.standfirst: Fed chief Jerome Powell said he will remain as a governor on the central bank's board after his term as chair ends on May 15.
items.content: Fed chief Jerome Powell said he will remain as a governor on the central bank's board after his term as chair ends on May 15.

items.title: Jerome Powell to stay on Fed board after central bank holds rates steady in defiance of Trump
items.standfirst: Chair planned to exit after inquiry into building renovations but will now oversee ‘remaining steps in the process’Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxThe US Federal Reserve chair, Jerome Powell, will remain on the central bank’s rate-setting board after his term as chair ends in May, a contentious move that signals continued uncertainty at the Fed.Powell made the announcement after the Fed board on Wednesday left interest rates unchanged for the third time this year, despite Donald Trump’s continued demands for rate cuts. Continue reading...
items.content: Chair planned to exit after inquiry into building renovations but will now oversee ‘remaining steps in the process’Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxThe US Federal Reserve chair, Jerome Powell, will remain on the central bank’s rate-setting board after his term as chair ends in May, a contentious move that signals continued uncertainty at the Fed.Powell made the announcement after the Fed board on Wednesday left interest rates unchanged for the third time this year, despite Donald Trump’s continued demands for rate cuts. items.title: FCC orders review of ABC licenses after Jimmy Kimmel’s Melania Trump joke
items.standfirst: Eight local broadcasting licenses under review after White House launched attack on late-night host over commentSign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxThe US’s top media watchdog announced on Tuesday that it is accelerating the review of eight local broadcasting licenses used by ABC, in a move critics see as a clear example of political and regulatory retribution against a disfavored broadcaster.The Federal Communications Commission’s (FCC) announcement comes after the White House launched a full-on attack against the ABC’s late-night host, Jimmy Kimmel, over a joke he made last week about Melania Trump. Continue reading...

items.title: 8 highlights of King Charles and Queen Camilla's visit to U.S.
items.standfirst: King Charles III and Queen Camilla's first trip to the U.S. as monarchs kicked off in Washington, D.C., for a state visit, where he delivered a historic speech to a joint meeting of Congress and presented President Trump with a WWII-era submarine bell. Here are some highlights of the monarchs' visit.
items.content: King Charles III and Queen Camilla's first trip to the U.S. as monarchs kicked off in Washington, D.C., for a state visit, where he delivered a historic speech to a joint meeting of Congress and presented President Trump with a WWII-era submarine bell. Here are some highlights of the monarchs' visit.

items.title: King Charles III visits Ground Zero, pledges 'enduring solidarity' with Americans
items.standfirst: King Charles III expressed “enduring solidarity with the American people” on Wednesday during a visit to New York’s 9/11 memorial, part of a state trip aimed at easing strained US-UK ties. The monarch and Queen Camilla laid white roses at the reflecting pools after earlier meeting President Donald Trump in Washington.
items.content: King Charles III expressed “enduring solidarity with the American people” on Wednesday during a visit to New York’s 9/11 memorial, part of a state trip aimed at easing strained US-UK ties. items.content: BBC reports from Kenya, home to Sabastian Sawe, the first man to run a marathon in under two hours.

clusters.title: French teen charged in Singapore over a vending machine straw-licking video
clusters.neutral_headline: French teen charged in Singapore over a vending machine straw-licking video
bullet_summary: Reported by NBC World, France24

items.title: French teen charged in Singapore over a vending machine straw-licking video
items.standfirst: A French teen is facing mischief and public nuisance charges in Singapore after posting a video on social media of himself licking a straw from an orange juice vending machine and then putting it back.
items.content: A French teen is facing mischief and public nuisance charges in Singapore after posting a video on social media of himself licking a straw from an orange juice vending machine and then putting it back.

items.title: French teen faces possible prison sentence for Singapore straw-licking video
items.standfirst: A French 18-year-old is facing mischief and public nuisance charges in Singapore after he posted a video to social media of him licking a straw from a vending machine and putting it back. The teen, who has not entered a plea, was charged after the company operating the juice machine filed a police report.
items.content: A French 18-year-old is facing mischief and public nuisance charges in Singapore after he posted a video to social media of him licking a straw from a vending machine and putting it back. The teen, who has not entered a plea, was charged after the company operating the juice machine filed a police report.

clusters.title: Saudi Arabia to withdraw LIV Golf funding
clusters.neutral_headline: Saudi Arabia to withdraw LIV Golf funding
bullet_summary: Reported by Sky News World, BBC Top Stories

items.title: Saudi Arabia to withdraw LIV Golf funding
items.standfirst: LIV Golf players and staff have been told that Saudi Arabia's sovereign wealth fund is withdrawing investment from its costly breakaway series after the 2026 season, Sky News understands.
items.content: LIV Golf players and staff have been told that Saudi Arabia's sovereign wealth fund is withdrawing investment from its costly breakaway series after the 2026 season, Sky News understands.

items.title: Saudi Arabia to stop funding LIV Golf next season
items.standfirst: Saudi Arabia will withdraw its multi-billion dollar backing of LIV Golf at the end of the season, plunging the future of the series into doubt.
items.content: Saudi Arabia will withdraw its multi-billion dollar backing of LIV Golf at the end of the season, plunging the future of the series into doubt.

clusters.title: Jewish agency warned of 'high' threat level ahead of Bondi shooting, report finds
clusters.neutral_headline: Bondi attack inquiry calls for greater security at Jewish events
bullet_summary: Reported by BBC World, Sky News World

items.title: Jewish agency warned of 'high' threat level ahead of Bondi shooting, report finds
items.standfirst: Gun reforms should be prioritised and policing around Jewish events increased, royal commission says.
items.content: Gun reforms should be prioritised and policing around Jewish events increased, royal commission says.

items.title: Bondi attack inquiry calls for greater security at Jewish events
items.standfirst: An inquiry into the Bondi Beach shooting has called for increased security at Jewish public events and further gun reforms in Australia.
items.content: An inquiry into the Bondi Beach shooting has called for increased security at Jewish public events and further gun reforms in Australia.

clusters.title: How a father and daughter duped NYC's art world with fake Warhols and Banksys
clusters.neutral_headline: How a father and daughter duped NYC's art world with fake Warhols and Banksys
bullet_summary: Federal prosecutors said Erwin Bankowski, 50, and Karolina Bankowska, 26, commissioned an artist in Poland to create at least 200 of the fakes and ultimately defrauded buyers of at least $2m
Reported by NPR News, Guardian World

items.title: How a father and daughter duped NYC's art world with fake Warhols and Banksys
items.standfirst: A father and daughter in New Jersey have pleaded guilty to running a years-long counterfeiting scheme to trick art galleries and auction houses into buying forged paintings
items.content: A father and daughter in New Jersey have pleaded guilty to running a years-long counterfeiting scheme to trick art galleries and auction houses into buying forged paintings(Image credit: Jake Offenhartz)

items.title: US father and daughter admit selling fake Picasso and Banksy works, duping art world
items.standfirst: Pair apologise in court after being accused of defrauding buyers including some of New York’s most prominent fine art auction housesA father and daughter in New Jersey have pleaded guilty to running a years-long counterfeiting scheme to trick art galleries and auction houses into buying forged paintings of works by prominent artists such as Andy Warhol, Banksy and Pablo Picasso.Federal prosecutors said Erwin Bankowski, 50, and Karolina Bankowska, 26, commissioned an artist in Poland to create at least 200 of the fakes and ultimately defrauded buyers of at least $2m. Continue reading...
items.content: Pair apologise in court after being accused of defrauding buyers including some of New York’s most prominent fine art auction housesA father and daughter in New Jersey have pleaded guilty to running a years-long counterfeiting scheme to trick art galleries and auction houses into buying forged paintings of works by prominent artists such as Andy Warhol, Banksy and Pablo Picasso.Federal prosecutors said Erwin Bankowski, 50, and Karolina Bankowska, 26, commissioned an artist in Poland to create at least 200 of the fakes and ultimately defrauded buyers of at least $2m. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Comey appears in court after his indictment for allegedly threatening Trump

bullet_summary: Reported by CBS News, BBC World

items.title: Comey appears in court after his indictment for allegedly threatening Trump
items.standfirst: Former FBI Director James Comey was indicted Tuesday for allegedly making threats against President Trump.
items.content: Former FBI Director James Comey was indicted Tuesday for allegedly making threats against President Trump.

items.title: Comey surrenders over charge of threatening Trump's life in Instagram post
items.standfirst: Prosecutors say a 2025 seashell photo posted by the former FBI director was a call for violence against Trump.
items.content: Prosecutors say a 2025 seashell photo posted by the former FBI director was a call for violence against Trump.

clusters.title: Iran crisis, World Cup tensions overshadow FIFA Congress in Vancouver
clusters.neutral_headline: Iran crisis, World Cup tensions overshadow FIFA Congress in Vancouver
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, Sky News World

items.title: Iran crisis, World Cup tensions overshadow FIFA Congress in Vancouver
items.standfirst: Football officials gather in Vancouver on Thursday for FIFA’s 76th Congress, weeks before the expanded World Cup kicks off in North America. War in Iran, logistics and Russia’s ban top the agenda. Iranian federation officials left Canada abruptly, casting a shadow over the meeting.
items.content: Football officials gather in Vancouver on Thursday for FIFA’s 76th Congress, weeks before the expanded World Cup kicks off in North America. War in Iran, logistics and Russia’s ban top the agenda. Iranian federation officials left Canada abruptly, casting a shadow over the meeting.

items.title: Iranian officials denied entry into Canada for FIFA meeting weeks before World Cup
items.standfirst: Iranian football federation officials have been denied access into Canada for a FIFA meeting just weeks before the start of the World Cup.
items.content: Iranian football federation officials have been denied access into Canada for a FIFA meeting just weeks before the start of the World Cup.
